_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates a hypothesis that reached 'verified' status through strong-tier verification (developer file-inspect of installed plugin files). Based on the originating recovery case anonymized. Live fix is permitted.
hypothesis: Commercial closed-source SEO plugin in the template inheritance chain renders a fallback canonical pointing to the navigation root (homepage) for blog detail routes, while the blog plugin also renders its own correct self-canonical, producing two competing canonical tags per blog detail page.
observed_effect: Blog detail routes show two <link rel=canonical> tags in live HTML: one Self (correct), one Homepage / (incorrect). 18 of 18 blog detail URLs affected. Category, product, and homepage URLs show exactly one canonical tag (correct).

supporting_evidence.observation: Cross-page-type comparison: blog detail pages have canonical_count=2, category/product/homepage have canonical_count=1. Pattern is route-specific, not theme-global.
Crawl marks 18 blog detail URLs as 'Nicht indexierbar - Canonicalised', 28 URL variants including case duplicates affected.
Blog plugin upstream repository contains a layout_head_meta_tags block override with a parent() call after its own canonical injection. Mechanically capable of producing the observed doubling.

Developer downloaded the installed commercial SEO plugin package from production server, inspected the plugin's Twig template files, identified the layout_head_meta_tags block override in the closed-source plugin that produces the homepage-fallback canonical for the blog plugin's detail routes. The blog plugin's parent() call lands at this override, not at Shopware Core.

excluded_alternatives.alternative: Custom theme base-layout injects a hardcoded homepage canonical on all storefront pages
excluded_alternatives.excluded_by: Live HTML cross-page-type comparison: category and homepage pages show only one canonical, ruling out a theme-global injection. Theme baseConfig inspection confirmed no canonical logic in theme.

excluded_alternatives.alternative: Open-source blog plugin alone is the canonical-doubling source
excluded_alternatives.excluded_by: Developer inspection of the production server confirmed that the open-source plugin's parent() call lands at a closed-source SEO plugin's override in the chain, not at Shopware Core. The open-source plugin is a trigger condition, not the producer of the homepage-fallback canonical value.

excluded_alternatives.alternative: Shopware Core canonical block produces the homepage fallback for blog routes due to missing metaInformation.canonicalLink
excluded_alternatives.excluded_by: Closed-source SEO plugin in the override chain intercepts the call before it reaches Shopware Core. Core is not actually rendering on these routes.

affected_components: custom-theme/Resources/views/storefront/layout/meta.html.twig (new file, overriding the closed-source plugin's block)
fix_scope.minimal_change_description: New Twig file in the custom theme that extends @CommercialSeoPlugin/storefront/layout/meta.html.twig and overrides layout_head_canonical to render only the self-canonical for the blog plugin's detail routes, suppressing the homepage fallback by not calling parent() for that route.

revert_trigger_conditions: canonical_count != 1 on any affected blog URL
canonical href value not self on any affected blog URL
homepage canonical reappears on any affected blog URL
category or product URL canonical state changes (cascade detection)
any functional check of the commercial SEO plugin fails (Rich Snippets disappear, Sitemap unreachable, Meta description missing)

operator_approval.approval_text: Theme-Override fuer blog-plugin-detail-routes deployen. Scope: 18 Blog-Detail-Routes. Mechanismus: layout_head_canonical-Override. Pre-Check 11:11 committed. Live-QA innerhalb 30 Min. Monitoring Stufe 1 aktiv.

settlement_gate_override.override_justification: Verified hypothesis with strongest-tier verification source (developer file inspect). Fix is Repair/Hygiene category. Reversibility under 5 minutes. 79% of 28-day click loss attributed to this single cause. Operator override decision documented per SEO_SETTLEMENT_GATE.md section 7.A.

status_history.reason: Live HTML cross-page-type comparison ruled out theme-global injection. Pattern is blog-route-specific.
Open-source plugin code read provided supporting evidence but at weak-tier strength. Status remains likely (attribution may be wrong).

Developer file-inspect of installed commercial SEO plugin on production server confirmed actual source. Two alternative hypotheses (theme-global, open-source-plugin-alone) explicitly excluded with cited evidence.
